---
title: sqlx Driver
description: Typed async query functions for sqlx.
---

import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

The `sqlx` driver generates typed async Rust functions using `sqlx::query_as` and `sqlx::query`. Each function accepts a `&sqlx::PgPool` and `.bind()` chains parameters in order.

---

## Configuration

```toml
[[targets]]
language = "rust"
schema   = "sql/schema.sql"
out      = "src/generated"
driver   = "sqlx"
```

---

## Generated files

| File | Contents |
|------|----------|
| `client.rs` | Module header comment |
| `<name>_queries.rs` | One file per `.sql` query file, containing typed functions |

---

## Query annotations

| Annotation | Return type | Use case |
|-----------|-------------|----------|
| `:one` | `Result<Option<T>, sqlx::Error>` | Fetch a single optional row |
| `:many` | `Result<Vec<T>, sqlx::Error>` | Fetch a list of rows |
| `:exec` | `Result<(), sqlx::Error>` | Insert / update / delete |
| `:execresult` | `Result<TResult, sqlx::Error>` | Mutation + rows affected count |

---

## Full example

**SQL queries** (`sql/queries/users.sql`):

```sql
-- name: GetUser :one
SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1;

-- name: ListUsers :many
SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE name ILIKE $1;

-- name: CreateUser :exec
INSERT INTO users (name, email, bio) VALUES ($1, $2, $3);

-- name: DeleteUser :execresult
DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1;
```

**Generated** (`users_queries.rs`):

```rust
// Code generated by sqlcx. DO NOT EDIT.

use sqlx;

pub const GET_USER_SQL: &str = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1";

#[derive(Debug, Clone, sqlx::FromRow)]
pub struct GetUserRow {
    pub id: i32,
    pub name: String,
    pub email: String,
    pub bio: Option<String>,
    pub status: String,
    pub tags: Option<Vec<String>>,
    pub created_at: chrono::NaiveDateTime,
}

pub async fn get_user(pool: &sqlx::PgPool, id: i32) -> Result<Option<GetUserRow>, sqlx::Error> {
    sqlx::query_as::<_, GetUserRow>(GET_USER_SQL)
        .bind(id)
        .fetch_optional(pool)
        .await
}

pub const LIST_USERS_SQL: &str = "SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE name ILIKE $1";

#[derive(Debug, Clone, sqlx::FromRow)]
pub struct ListUsersRow {
    pub id: i32,
    pub name: String,
    pub email: String,
}

pub async fn list_users(pool: &sqlx::PgPool, name: &str) -> Result<Vec<ListUsersRow>, sqlx::Error> {
    sqlx::query_as::<_, ListUsersRow>(LIST_USERS_SQL)
        .bind(name)
        .fetch_all(pool)
        .await
}

pub const CREATE_USER_SQL: &str = "INSERT INTO users (name, email, bio) VALUES ($1, $2, $3)";

pub async fn create_user(pool: &sqlx::PgPool, name: &str, email: &str, bio: &str) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
    sqlx::query(CREATE_USER_SQL)
        .bind(name)
        .bind(email)
        .bind(bio)
        .execute(pool)
        .await
        .map(|_| ())
}

pub const DELETE_USER_SQL: &str = "DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1";

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct DeleteUserResult {
    pub rows_affected: u64,
}

pub async fn delete_user(pool: &sqlx::PgPool, id: i32) -> Result<DeleteUserResult, sqlx::Error> {
    let result = sqlx::query(DELETE_USER_SQL)
        .bind(id)
        .execute(pool)
        .await?;
    Ok(DeleteUserResult { rows_affected: result.rows_affected() })
}
```

---

## Using the generated code

```rust
use sqlx::PgPool;

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
    let pool = PgPool::connect("postgres://localhost/myapp").await?;

    // Insert a user
    create_user(&pool, "Alice", "alice@example.com", "").await?;

    // Fetch a single user — returns None if not found
    if let Some(user) = get_user(&pool, 1).await? {
        println!("{} <{}>", user.name, user.email);
    }

    // Fetch a list
    let users = list_users(&pool, "%alice%").await?;
    for u in &users {
        println!("{} {}", u.id, u.email);
    }

    // Delete and check row count
    let res = delete_user(&pool, 1).await?;
    println!("deleted {} row(s)", res.rows_affected);

    Ok(())
}
```

---

## Parameter types

String parameters use `&str` (borrowed), while numeric and boolean parameters are passed by value:

| SQL type | Function parameter type |
|---------|------------------------|
| `TEXT`, `VARCHAR`, `UUID` | `&str` |
| `BYTEA` | `&[u8]` |
| `INTEGER`, `SERIAL` | `i32` |
| `BIGINT`, `BIGSERIAL` | `i64` |
| `REAL`, `FLOAT`, `NUMERIC` | `f64` |
| `BOOLEAN` | `bool` |
| `TIMESTAMP` | `chrono::NaiveDateTime` |

---

## Cargo dependencies

```toml
[dependencies]
sqlx    = { version = "0.8", features = ["postgres", "chrono", "runtime-tokio-native-tls"] }
chrono  = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }
tokio   = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
```
